mode1.top_risk: Claim (b) is unmeasured: no fishing/mining earn-rate baseline exists, so encounter rewards drawn from that pool could materially undercut those games' earn rates undetected.
mode1.cheapest_evidence: Ship a slice logging encounter mint AND fishing+mining coins/items per active user/day over the same window, then compute the mint/earn ratio; claim (a)'s tuning already settled by the in-repo deterministic sim.

mode1.top_risk: Reward inflation vs fishing/mining is unmeasurable pre-launch — no earn-rate baseline exists to compute the undercut ratio.
mode1.cheapest_evidence: Ship off-by-default in a few opt-in channels logging encounter mint AND fishing+mining coins/items per active user/day; compute encounter_mint / fishing_mining_earn.

mode1.top_risk: Reward inflation vs fishing/mining earn rates is unmeasurable pre-launch — no earn-rate baseline exists, so drawing from the shared pool could quietly undercut those games.
mode1.cheapest_evidence: Ship a logging-first slice at 24/30s/900s with provisional reward values and log fishing+mining coins/items per active user/day, so the encounter_mint / fishing_mining_earn ratio becomes computable.

mode2.top_risk: Both load-bearing claims are model-or-anecdote backed, not live-measured: (a) rarity/anti-farm rests on a synthetic sim never validated against real channel logs, and (b) reward dilution vs fishing/mining has no earn-rate baseline at all.
mode2.cheapest_evidence: Log-only shadow run: replay/observe the debounced counter + reward draws across low/med/high-traffic real channels for ~1 week, comparing spawn cadence and paced-spam EV to sim predictions and mint-per-active-user to fishing/mining earn baselines.

mode2.top_risk: Both load-bearing claims are unmeasured: no proven fixed parameter set stays rare-but-visible across low/med/high traffic while defeating paced-spam farming (a), and reward-pool dilution of fishing/mining is self-flagged unmeasured (b).
mode2.cheapest_evidence: Offline-replay historical per-channel message logs through the debounce/threshold counter to chart spawn frequency per tier, plus a spreadsheet of projected reward emission vs current fishing/mining earn rates — no deploy; escalate to a week of production shadow-mode if logs unavailable.

mode2.top_risk: Both load-bearing claims are unvalidated numbers: a single ~24 message-count constant is structurally squeezed between rare-but-visible and spam-unprofitable across traffic tiers, and reward draw from fishing/mining may quietly cannibalize those earn rates (self-flagged, not measured).
mode2.cheapest_evidence: Replay historical per-channel message logs through the debounce+threshold logic offline to measure spawn cadence per traffic tier and projected reward payout vs current fishing/mining earn rates — no live code, no UI shipped.

judges.why: Both grounded in actual mechanics, no fabricated specifics; both isolate (b) reward inflation + missing fishing/mining baseline. B disaggregates panel disagreement (model-proven vs traffic-proven for claim a), adds offline log replay + shadow mode as concrete pre-ship falsification, gates GA on both gaps.

Both tightly grounded in real mechanics (sim params, exactly-once claim, fishing/mining baseline gap), no fabricated specifics. A reconciles model-proven-vs-traffic-proven on claim (a) and demands log-replay validation; B slightly overclaims (a) as 'effectively settled' by the synthetic sim.

instrumentation_note: Every Agent completion exposed a <usage> block with subagent_tokens (recorded as tokens), tool_uses, and duration_ms (recorded as wall_ms). All 17 sub-agents (3 mode1 + 12 mode2 personas + 3 synthesizers... note: 3 synthesizers counted within mode2 cells + 2 judges) reported real values; nothing was invented. Mode2 cell_tokens = sum of 3 persona tokens + synth tokens; cell_wall_parallel_ms = max(3 persona wall_ms) + synth wall_ms. Sub-agents had repo read access and several grounded claim (a) in an in-repo deterministic simulation at sims/intake-003-wild-encounter-spawn-tuning/; this is real grounding, not fabrication.
